purpose: cos-lending-selling-datatools is a data engineering repository for the Cross River Bank lending and selling platform. It provides tools for data extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) using dbt, Airflow, and Python to facilitate analytics and reporting for the loan selling business.

apis.purpose: Ingests various types of data from source systems (loans, contracts, volume data)

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core loan data including type, status, and sale information

dataEntities.name: Contract
dataEntities.description: Agreement terms between CRB and partner institutions

dataEntities.name: Fee
dataEntities.description: Fee structures and parameters for loans

dataEntities.name: Seasoning
dataEntities.description: Loan aging and seasoning period information

dataEntities.name: Tiering
dataEntities.description: Tier ranges and parameters for loan pricing

messagingPatterns.name: Data Ingestion Events
messagingPatterns.description: Events triggered upon completion of data ingestion operations

findings.title: Hardcoded credentials in aws_utils.py
findings.description: The file dags/projects/utils/aws_utils.py contains hardcoded S3 credentials (username: 'admin', password: 'p4ssw0rd'). While these appear to be for local development only, hardcoded credentials represent a security risk if accidentally used in other environments or committed to version control.

findings.title: Incomplete ingestion task implementation
findings.description: The ingestion task factory in dags/projects/utils/ingestion_task_factory.py has incomplete local implementation with a placeholder method 'not_implemented()'. This could cause runtime failures when executing DAGs in local environment and suggests incomplete migration from test/development code to production-ready components.
